papers.title: The Scalability of Transformer Attention: A Critical Survey of Long-Context Mechanisms

annotations.quote: most deployed pipelines still truncate, chunk, or summarise their inputs rather than attending over them directly
annotations.contextBefore: At the context lengths now demanded of production systems, it dominates both the training budget and the inference latency, and it is the reason
annotations.note: Name two or three of these production pipelines, or soften 'most' — the claim is currently unsupported.

annotations.quote: It is sometimes claimed that linear attention is equivalent to softmax attention in the limit of an exact feature map.
annotations.contextBefore: the feature map's positivity assumptions are stressed.
annotations.contextAfter: This is true only in a limit that no deployed feature map approaches
annotations.note: Attribute this claim — who makes it? A citation would let the reader weigh the equivalence result you are qualifying.
